mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 8.0 percent, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 8.0, or the signal shows no predictive power over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint acceleration of real GDP growth and housing starts indicates synchronized expansion in business investment and residential construction, which amplifies aggregate demand and pushes S&P 500 forward returns higher over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 0.02 compared to a single-proxy baseline model over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, a sustained rise in the industrial production index reflects rebuilding supply chains and capacity utilization, which supports corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns turn negative when INDPRO rises above its 12-month moving average during recovery periods, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances indicate increasing household leverage and near-term spending momentum, which directly supports retail and service sector earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The monthly change in RSAFS shows no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns (p-value > 0.10) across multiple decades of data.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 0.50 percent, it signals the end of the zero-lower-bound environment and triggers repricing of duration risk, which compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Equity markets continue to rally for more than 60 days after FEDFUNDS breaches 0.50%, or the signal produces a positive Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market recovery, which reduces wage-price pressures while boosting aggregate demand, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a baseline market model in out-of-sample directional accuracy, or the interaction term becomes statistically insignificant when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: In early-cycle expansion regimes, a month-over-month increase in housing starts acts as a leading indicator for broader economic momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as construction and related sectors rally.
failure_condition: HOUST increases coincide with negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of observations during identified expansion phases, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.5 over a 10-year test period.
